page: OVHcloud c3-8 is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It does not include a GPU.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench text processing tests placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and Geekbench multi-core scores fall within the average range. However, static web serving throughput and prompt processing for medium-sized LLM models are weak, placing in the bottom 25% of tested servers.

Given its dedicated AMD EPYC cores and low RAM-to-vCPU ratio of 1:2, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. Tradeoffs include limited memory capacity and lower web serving throughput. It is appropriate for single-threaded processing tasks, compilation, and moderate computational workloads rather than high-throughput web serving or large-scale database hosting.
description: OVHcloud c3-8 is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 4 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain average. Static web serving throughput is weak. This hardware profile provides a resource-dense option for compute-bound tasks that do not require extensive memory, making it suitable for software compilation, single-threaded processing, and moderate computational workloads.
